column1: # Adapted 2026-07-15 by Alfanumrik (Cusiosense Learning India Private Limited) from
# and ../vendor/NOTICE. Changes: IM (Illustrative Mathematics) branch of P-M1 removed in
# favor of a curriculum-neutral arc with a 'curriculum-confirmed' conditional; US
# "standard" reframed as NCERT learning objective. Layer this file ON TOP of
# ncert-lesson-planning.csv for math lessons. Lines starting with '#' are comments.

column3: Instructional model is coherent and unbranded
column4: If the teacher named a specific published curriculum or textbook series, the lesson matches that curriculum's instructional model. If no curriculum is named: any coherent research-based structure passes — e.g., Launch → Explore → Discuss → Synthesize, or an NCERT activity-led arc — so long as no curriculum-proprietary terminology appears. Fail = proprietary routine names for a teacher who never named that curriculum, or a named curriculum's instructional model ignored.
column5: Adapted: upstream IM-specific branch removed; principle (match confirmed curriculum / stay unbranded otherwise) kept.

column4: Math misconceptions must name the wrong equation form vs. the correct one (e.g., 'student writes 45 + ? = 18 instead of 45 − 18 = ?'), not describe a general confusion pattern. Pass = at least 2 of 3 misconceptions include a specific wrong form. Fail = all misconceptions are general pattern descriptions.

column3: Discuss phase with student-to-student talk moves
column4: The lesson includes a dedicated Discuss/Synthesize phase with at least 10 minutes allocated, embedding at least one NAMED talk move (e.g., turn-and-talk, revoicing, agree/disagree, partner compare, think-pair-share) — and at least one named move is a student-to-student interaction. Fail = no discussion phase, under 10 minutes, no named talk move, or a discussion that is entirely teacher-led questioning.

column3: Problem set covers the objective's structural variety
column4: FIRST enumerate the structural cases of the target learning objective yourself (e.g., unknown positions for operations word problems, transformation types, proportional vs. non-proportional cases). THEN verify every enumerated case appears in the practice problems or exit ticket. List the enumeration and where each case appears in the explanation. Fail = any structural case of the objective is absent from the problem set.

column3: Objective's full numerical scope is used
column4: If the learning objective specifies rational numbers, at least one problem uses decimals or fractions. If the objective specifies 'within 100', problems use numbers throughout that range, not just single digits. Pass = numerical scope matches the objective. Fail = only whole numbers when rational required, or only small numbers when the objective specifies a larger range.

Each student task's answer surface fits its activity: a task asking students to compute, draw, partition, or build a model gets open space or the diagram itself; a task asking for a composed sentence or explanation may use ruled lines at any grade. Fail = ruled writing lines under a task whose expected work is drawing or computation. Judge the student materials only; name the mismatched task in the explanation.
